selectionProcess:
1. Resume Shortlisting
2. Technical Test (Fluid mechanics, Aero, CFD basics)
3. Technical Interview (Focus on projects, boundary layer theory, wind tunnel testing)
4. HR Interview (Why automotive, work culture fits)
summary: Worked with the Aero-Thermal team on streamlining the rear chassis of an upcoming electric vehicle. The experience was incredibly rich, with access to high-performance computing clusters for CFD simulations. The mentors were super approachable and guided me through advanced meshing techniques. It was a steep learning curve but highly rewarding.
takeaways: Brush up on Fluid Mechanics (ME 201) and boundary layer calculations. Prior experience with ANSYS Fluent or OpenFOAM is a massive plus. Be ready to explain all details of your fluid-related projects.

selectionProcess:
1. Resume shortlisting based on CGPA and leadership roles
2. Case Interviews (2 rounds with Consultants/Project Leaders)
3. Partner Round (Fitment & Case summary)
summary: Assisted in the digital transformation of an Indian steel manufacturer. My day-to-day involved data modeling, market research, and designing client slides. The work hours were long (often 12-14 hours/day), but the exposure to C-suite clients and strategic decision making was unmatched. Excellent learning environment.
takeaways: Start practicing case prep at least 2 months before. Read 'Case in Point' and form a case group with friends to practice mock interviews. Show structured thinking and comfortable number crunching under pressure.
